mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuation multiples and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any two-year rolling window, or twenty-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p greater than 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession risk, triggering institutional risk-off positioning that depresses S&P 500 prices over the subsequent week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises greater than 0.2 percentage points in one month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive five-day returns in more than fifty percent of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and industrial production indicates robust aggregate demand and capacity utilization, supporting corporate earnings growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative Rank IC over any three-year rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production within a supply-constrained environment signals firm pricing power and expanding profit margins, systematically lifting equity valuations over a one-month forward horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production increases coincide with S&P 500 declines in more than sixty percent of observations over a five-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in retail sales forecasts immediate demand weakness and inventory drawdowns, prompting algorithmic and institutional selling that depresses S&P 500 prices over the next five trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls greater than five percent month-over-month
failure_condition: Retail sales drops are followed by positive S&P 500 five-day returns in more than fifty-five percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real gross domestic product growth expands the aggregate earnings base and reduces recession probabilities, systematically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: GDP growth acceleration correlates with negative S&P 500 sixty-day returns in more than fifty percent of rolling five-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous elevated inflation and high policy rates compress equity risk premiums and suppress valuation multiples through higher discount rates, generating negative forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: High inflation and high rates coexist with positive S&P 500 twenty-day returns in more than sixty percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly employment growth during a post-shock recovery phase signals wage-driven consumption expansion and fiscal multiplier effects, boosting near-term equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Employment growth exceeds two hundred thousand in recovery phases but S&P 500 five-day returns remain negative in more than fifty percent of cases.
